Barbra Roberts is beloved by everyone, accomplished in every career, and one of the most powerful people in the world. Step into the Dreamhouse.

His social media has recently made that increasingly difficult to hide, and the public is starting to realise what those closest to them have always known: Octavia is the talent. Orange is growing up in the middle of all of this, wanting for nothing and understanding very little of it.

Lance got this apartment thanks to a chain of connections so improbable it barely makes sense to retell: his dad's uncle's cousin's wife's yoga instructor's niece needed to sell fast and Lance happened to be standing in the right place at the right time. What a stroke of luck huh?

Tanya works two jobs and holds the family together while Rue tears through her teenage years like someone who is in tremendous pain and has decided to be loud about it. Maya is younger and still watching, still learning what a mother looks like under pressure.

He dances for a living and operates in some of the city's shadier corners on the side, and through all of it he still believes, genuinely and without embarrassment, that the right person is out there. In a city built on grit and compromise, that kind of hope is either very brave or very foolish. Probably both.

Araminta was bitten, not born, and every high-born vampire in Forgotten Hollow makes sure she knows it. She married into one of the oldest bloodlines and that has done nothing to soften the judgment, which is why she needs her social standing to improve before anything else. Ishmael grew up as the last of his bloodline and simply loves having a family again. Elowen is their teenager, quietly seeing a werewolf, which Araminta does not need right now.

Wisteria is not just surviving in Forgotten Hollow, she is winning. Her ancestors left her a protection strong enough to keep the vampires at bay and she has spent her life weaponising it,attepting to stay young, staying sharp, and making sure she gets to them before they ever get to her. The garlic on her porch is not decorative.

Nigel always dreamed of being a camp director and here he is, living it. HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Strength Through Union has been the Darong family motto for generations, and it has held. Jawara has spent years earning favour across the kingdom through philanthropy and grace, and the path to the crown seemed smooth until her husband died suddenly and the scrutiny that followed started asking whether this perfect family was really as spotless as it appeared. Jawara is pressing forward. The family is closing ranks behind her.

Layla and Kirk are both photographers, which is what brought them together and also, quietly, what pulls them apart. Layla chases moments. Kirk builds narratives. They have the same eye and a completely different idea of what to do with it, and the creative tension between them has a way of bleeding into everything else.

Ekene is a modest artisan who has spent his later years raising his granddaughter Afua after her mother's passing. The father? Well. Ekene says he was simply too different from the rest of them and that it was mutually agreed Afua would grow up an Ananou, living a regular life. People in Dambele still gossip about it. Some have noticed the donations that appear in the Ananous' mailbox from time to time. Ekene and Afua are playful, warm, and welcoming, as long as you do not mind the pranks.
Tuan is doing it alone: two very small children, a household to hold together, and whatever it takes to make the days work. It is not glamorous and he is not asking for credit.

Cordelia Thebe V came home from years at sea and settled down, which surprised everyone including possibly herself. Now married to Thiago Abrantes and well-positioned in the race to the throne, she has everything a candidate could want and the restlessness of someone who has always wanted to be somewhere else instead.

Aloisio learned early that the ends justify the means and has been living by that ever since. He moved to Verdemar from Dambele with ambition and a clear eye for opportunity, and whether he ends up fighting inequality or exploiting it depends entirely on which one is more useful at the time.

Ermelinda has achieved what she set out to achieve: her son married well, the family has ascended, and she could retire. She has not retired. Sofia is her teenage daughter, still naive enough to stumble across court scandals without fully understanding what she's found, which makes her either an asset or a liability depending on the day.

Daniel and Laura are so reliably normal that Jisoo has spent her whole childhood trying to be anything but. She tries everything, joins everything, and knows far more popular people than anyone looking at her from the outside would expect. Baby Jiwon has just arrived and has not yet formed an opinion on any of this.

Tybalt has the money, the house, and the highest noble ranking in Bellacorde. He also wants to be king, which he considers a natural next step. His three adult children refuse to settle down and marry, which is the one stain on an otherwise perfect image: Beatrice who could have anyone but wants something meaningful, Celeste who takes the family motto with an earnestness Tybalt finds baffling, and Virgilio who calls high society a collection of hypocrites and is not entirely wrong.

Juliette followed her heart, lost her noble ranking, and lost most of her fortune, and has spent every day since deciding it was worth it. She and Romeo are settled now, Dante is growing up with his father's kindness, and Juliette has started to think about what it would take to reclaim what was taken from her.

Tage is a young lawyer in Bellacorde who still believes the law means something, which is either the most admirable quality in the room or the most fragile one depending on who you ask. The hope in his eyes has not dimmed yet. Give it time.

Life in Bellacorde was not always this comfortable for Ixia. Working for Kason has a way of solving certain problems very efficiently, and as long as she keeps doing what she does and keeps her mouth shut about the rest of it, the apartment stays nice and the money stays good. So far so good.

Rumour has it that Olive Specter's prior husbands all met unfortunate ends. Layne Coffin, founder and sole member of the band Withering Widdershins, is her current fiance and has apparently decided not to ask too many questions about that. Nyon's parentage is similarly not up for discussion. What is clear is that the Grim Reaper checks in on this household with unusual frequency, and not all of it is explained by Olive's romantic history.

HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: The Frumps are a family who share the Addams family's love of the dark, the strange, and the deeply unconventional. They gather warmly, celebrate loudly, and participate in the community with an enthusiasm that most of the community finds either charming or unsettling depending on the occasion.

Ophelia sells strange plants from Teardrop Cottage and has a gentle, floral disposition that sits slightly sideways against the broader Frump aesthetic. Something is developing between her and a spellcaster who has been finding reasons to visit Ravenwood with increasing regularity, is there romance in the air? or is one of this flowers poisonous?

It and Margaret are the ... living members of this Addams branch. Margaret, who came into the Addams family through an unexpected love story and embraced every peculiar custom with an open heart, and Itt, their son, born under circumstances the obstetrician also did not anticipate. Amenia and That Addams watch over them.

HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Morriva wants all the magic and all the power, and she is mean enough to take it seriously. She has surrounded herself with the most dangerous names in the occult world and is working toward something that nobody outside Mourning Mist Manor is fully aware of yet.

Gomez and Morticia are the warm, terrifying centre of Ravenwood. There are ancestors who still show up for dinner, a zombie butler, witch aunts, and enough history in these walls to fill several lifetimes. Everyone can see the dead, which here is less a gift and more a guest list.

Thalia has never quite fit anywhere, which makes a certain amount of sense when your biology involves laying eggs. Growing up as a descendant of the Easter Hare means a childhood of being the strange one, the unexplainable one, the one other kids look at sideways. She found her footing eventually, became a dancer, learned to inhabit her body with confidence in the specific context of her work, and remains quietly, persistently awkward everywhere else.

Brielle has watched her mother become a stranger and her father become something worse, and she has grown to resent both of them with equal energy. She is saving money, making her own plans, and leaving the moment she can.

Kai got the apartment the way most things happen to Kai: someone wanted to do something nice for him and could not fully explain why afterward. A director he had briefly met mentioned a unit opening up to a producer who mentioned it to an assistant who passed it along and there Kai was, keys in hand, Dante moving in two weeks later because the rent split made sense. Kai has that effect on situations.

HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Kason found Kitty and Min-Jun at a low point, offered them work and a place to land, and now they have an apartment in the Arts Quarter and employment that keeps the lights on. Nobody signed anything that said they owed him. Nobody had to. They both know what being on the street looks like and they both know who pulled them off it, and that is a debt that does not need to be written down to be very, very real.

Axara grew up somewhere considerably smaller and quieter than San Myshuno, which is exactly why she left. The city was where the work was and the work was where she needed to be, and she traded rural quiet for an Arts Quarter studio and never looked back. She is a tech guru who happens to practice the craft, modernised enough that most people she works with would never guess the latter.

Liam's sister Emma is an A.W.E. actress. Liam is a stripper, which is not what their parents planned but is also not entirely their fault given how little those parents invested in either of them. Somewhere out there Liam has an older brother his parents abandoned before he was even born, a fact Emma has spent years telling him to let go of. He has not let go of it. Marisol, who has her own complicated relationship with moving on, has been helping him look. They are not a couple. They are two people with too much history and not enough answers, sharing a studio in the Arts Quarter and making it work.

Dafne is an A.W.E. producer who has learned that the fastest way to get what you need in this industry is to make yourself useful in ways that go beyond the job description. She is good at her work and equally good at knowing when a favour is being offered and what to offer in return.
Vernon is an A.W.E. director on his way up and has made himself useful to the kinds of people who make that climb easier. The company he keeps says as much as his résumé does.

Orin is on his way up and he has arranged his life accordingly. The penthouse came before the fame, which is either delusional or visionary, and the answer to that question is being settled in real time. He is ambitious, he is talented, and San Myshuno is exactly the right city for someone who has decided, with complete sincerity, that obscurity is temporary.

They are both fashion designers living in the only neighbourhood that truly understands what that means, and they both arrived with something to shake up. Bayani has the experience and the eye and the quiet authority of someone who has been in the room long enough to know which battles matter. Kemal has the intensity and the ambition and the specific energy of someone who has not been in the room long enough to be careful yet. Between them, the Fashion District is about to hear from both.

HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Eugene and Mabel have been on Courtyard Lane so long that the neighbourhood has grown up around them. They opened the daycare because they had warmth to spare, and somewhere along the way started raising children who were not entirely their own. Some of those children stayed in their lives long after. Some of them grew up, married, and lost their spouses to a killer nobody has identified yet, and Eugene and Mabel grieve each one as if they were blood, because to them they are.

Igor and Maria are both academics doing everything well, and Manara is their daughter who has joined every extracurricular available to her and is excelling at all of them. The household is impressively high-functioning.

Eze proposed before he had the money and Zainab said yes before she had the degree, and somehow they have both decided that's fine, that the life they want is worth building from the bottom. He is laying the foundation of the business brick by brick. She is finishing school between long nights and longer prayers. There is something quietly stubborn about this household, a refusal to wait for the right moment that makes the whole thing feel like it's already working.
Empty

Jordan has lived on Courtyard Lane long enough to know which curtains twitch and whose car is parked where it shouldn't be. He is a journalist, quiet and pleasant, the kind of neighbour everyone trusts because he never seems to be paying attention. He is always paying attention.

Faithe and Sigifred have always rewarded beauty over substance, which is why Victoria grew up knowing she was the favourite and Naida grew up knowing she had to be smarter than everyone in the room just to be noticed. She is. The café looks lovely. The family underneath it is a little less so.

Hendrik is a devoted husband and father in a household full of creative warmth. He and Anneliese have built something genuinely happy here. Jorien is their teenager, Johannah Ridley's closest friend, and quietly, painfully in love with Johannah's younger sister Jade, lets hope no drama ensues.

Vincent lost his wife to a murderer and has been holding Sloane together ever since with the determination of a man who cannot afford to fall apart. Sloane is doing the same for him, and in between she is also investigating her mother's murder, because she is her mother's daughter and she is not going to wait for someone else to find the answer.

Evelyn Brown is a loving mother, a good neighbour, a dependable presence on Courtyard Lane, and a serial killer. Brian has no idea. Mia worships her. Nobody in the neighbourhood has any idea, and the bodies that have piled up quietly over the years are connected to a family called the Heartwells, whose adopted children kept losing their spouses one by one, and nobody has put it together yet.

Constance King peaked in high school and has been recreating it through her daughter ever since. She is the cool mom, the one who buys the drinks and knows all the gossip and cannot see the difference between reliving her youth and raising a child. Edward's money funds all of it. Regina has absorbed every lesson her mother ever taught her and turned it into a social operation. She is the HBIC of the cheer squad, Jodie Ridley's chief rival, and Taehun Yoh's other option, and she plays all three of those cards with the precision of someone who has been practising since she could talk.

Ethel filled the space her husband left with Ethan, and Ethan filled the space his mother created with every woman who was not Kamila. Kamila fills the space left by all of it with food and forgiveness and the kind of love that keeps absorbing damage it should not have to absorb. Johannah is watching all of this and building toward something quieter. Jade is goth and in love with Sloane Obaja and putting distance between herself and the house where she can. Jodie is at war with Regina King over a boy who has not decided anything. Jonathan is Ethel's favourite and has learned to enjoy it. They are a family that loves each other enormously and wounds each other constantly.

They are enjoying the particular satisfaction of parents who know they did something right.

Abd-al-Alim and Amal Al-Saud have made an art form of marrying their daughters well. Beautiful daughters placed into wealthy households have been the family's primary strategy for generational advancement, and it has worked exactly as intended.

The murders in Willow Creek created a fear that needed a face to calm it, and Perrin Meylor was ready with the right words at exactly the right time. He won his seat on the safety vote and has been the local politician ever since, which is a story that would be more inspiring if it were not so calculated.

NOTES:
Worship of the watchers
[An unseen hand moves all we do,
The Watcher sees our whole life through.
We paint and build and strive to please
The one who guides us as they please.
Our lives are theirs to shape and keep 
We wake by will, and sleep by sleep.]
HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Pastor Malak Varron has a megachurch, a devoted congregation, and a lifestyle that invites questions his sermons are specifically designed not to answer. The money flows in and very little of it flows back out, and the people who might ask about it are the same people he has made himself indispensable to.

HOUSEHOLD DESCRIPTION: Jason's father was the blueprint for exactly the kind of man Jason became: controlling, chest-forward, allergic to softness.his wife Needles is in prison, his kids Jett and Joy are at boarding school. Jason is alone in a tiny home in WindSlar with a household that technically still exists and an approach to fatherhood that has never once made any of this easier.

The Schulzs are blood relatives of the Heartwells, which is what brought them to Willow Creek in the first place and what they left behind when life pulled them toward Windenburg instead. They settled into the Lighthouse with their children, Rose, Damien, Marine and Elliot, before the neighbourhood they came from started losing people. One spouse at a time, quietly, with no explanation. The Schulzs were already gone by then. They do not know how lucky that is.
